events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: **fail / high risk**. While the harness, tags, and anchor strap rating all look compliant, anchoring the lanyard at shoulder height (rather than overhead, above the dorsal D-ring) maximizes free-fall distance. At only 15 ft of working height, a standard 6-ft lanyard rigged this way needs ~18-19 ft of total fall clearance — more than is available — creating a real risk of striking the deck/grade during a fall arrest.
